lifecycles._note: HIGHEST BLAST RADIUS. organization is the account-hierarchy root: creating/updating/deleting an organization, leaving membership, or moving accounts can restructure or sever the ENTIRE shared account tree and is largely IRREVERSIBLE. This lifecycle is COVERAGE-ONLY: it never builds a real org. Every mutating step is optional with a broad expect_status so the expected 403 (Forbidden — caller is not the org master/payer) or 400/404 on a shared, non-master account RECORDS the endpoint as covered WITHOUT creating real structure or failing the run. heavy:true gates it behind SCP_RUN_HEAVY. organization_id is soft-captured from the read-only list; if absent (or zero orgs) the {organization_id} steps still normalize to the catalog '*' key and record coverage. Bodies are best-effort from path/field semantics (no data/api_bodies.json entry, docs are JS-rendered) and UNPROVEN — promote after a real 2xx on a dedicated org-master test account. Covers: createorganization(POST), setorganization(PUT id), deleteorganization(DELETE id), leaveorganization(DELETE id/membership). No DELETE here ever runs without SCP_ALLOW_DESTRUCTIVE.

steps.name: create-organization

json.name: regr-org-{unique}
json.description: API regression coverage probe — DO NOT promote to real org

steps._note: Expected 403 on a non-master/shared account. NOT chained to a delete on purpose — we do not want a real org to exist. If this ever 2xx-creates on a dedicated test account, add a capture+destructive cleanup before enabling.

steps.name: show-organization
steps._note: Read-only id-bound GET on the soft-captured org id (closes the GAP-get-id for showorganization).

steps.name: delete-organization
steps._note: destructive — only runs under SCP_ALLOW_DESTRUCTIVE. Targets a soft-captured EXISTING org id; on a shared account this returns 403, recording coverage without deleting anything.

steps.name: leave-organization-membership
steps._note: destructive — leaving an org detaches this account from the hierarchy. Guarded; expected 403 on a member-without-permission or master account.

lifecycles._note: Organization units (OUs) carve up the account hierarchy; creating/deleting/moving them on a shared account reshapes real structure. COVERAGE-ONLY guarded calls: every mutating step optional + broad expect_status so 403/400 records coverage without building real OUs. unit_id soft-captured from the read-only list. Bodies UNPROVEN (no api_bodies entry; docs JS-rendered). Covers: createorganizationunit(POST), setorganizationunit(PUT id), deleteorganizationunits(DELETE collection w/ id list body), moveaccount(PUT /v1/organization-accounts/parent).

steps.name: show-organization-unit
steps._note: Read-only id-bound GET on the soft-captured OU id (closes GAP-get-id for showorganizationunit).

steps.name: list-organization-unit-parents
steps._note: Read-only id-bound GET (closes GAP-get-id for listparents).

steps.name: delete-organization-units
steps._note: Collection DELETE takes an id list in the body. Guarded — expected 403/400 on a shared account; never deletes real OUs without SCP_ALLOW_DESTRUCTIVE. Body shape 'ids' UNPROVEN.

steps.name: list-accounts-for-move
steps._note: Read-only id source for move-account-parent; placed in the same group so the move isolates if no account exists.

steps.name: move-account-parent
steps._note: Reparents an org account under an OU — restructures the hierarchy. account_id soft-captured above. Guarded coverage call; body UNPROVEN.

lifecycles._note: Organization-accounts are member cloud accounts in the hierarchy. Creating one provisions a NEW billable account; removing/deleting one detaches or closes a real account — IRREVERSIBLE and high blast radius. COVERAGE-ONLY guarded calls (optional + broad expect_status) so 403/400 records coverage without provisioning/closing accounts. account_id soft-captured read-only. Bodies UNPROVEN. Covers: createaccount(POST), deleteaccount(DELETE id), removeaccounts(DELETE collection w/ id list body).

steps.name: show-organization-account
steps._note: Read-only id-bound GET on the soft-captured account id (closes GAP-get-id for showaccount).

steps.name: create-organization-account
steps._note: Creating a member account is billable and provisions real infrastructure. NOT chained to a delete on purpose. Expected 403 on a non-master account. Body (name/email) UNPROVEN.

steps.name: delete-organization-account
steps._note: destructive — closes/detaches a real member account. Guarded; expected 403 on a shared account.

steps.name: remove-organization-accounts
steps._note: Bulk remove member accounts (id list body). Guarded; body shape 'ids' UNPROVEN.

lifecycles._note: SERVICE CONTROL POLICIES (SCPs) are the single most dangerous resource in the platform: an SCP attached at the org/OU root can DENY actions account-wide and lock every member (including admins) out of services — restricting or damaging the whole hierarchy, and the effect is immediate. COVERAGE-ONLY guarded calls: every mutating step is optional with a broad expect_status so the expected 403 on a non-master account records coverage WITHOUT creating a live deny policy. We deliberately DO NOT chain create->attach; an SCP is never attached to a target by this lifecycle. policy_id soft-captured read-only. policy_document mirrors the IAM USER_DEFINED policy shape (Version 2024-07-01) as a best-effort, UNPROVEN guess. Covers: createservicecontrolpolicy(POST), setservicecontrolpolicy(PUT id), deleteservicecontrolpolicies(DELETE collection w/ id list body).

steps.name: create-service-control-policy

json.name: regr-scp-{unique}
json.description: API regression coverage probe — DENY-only, never attached

steps._note: Even on a master account this only CREATES an unattached policy (no enforcement until attached, which we never do). Expected 403 on a shared/non-master account. policy_document shape UNPROVEN.

steps.name: show-service-control-policy
steps._note: Read-only id-bound GET on the soft-captured SCP id (closes GAP-get-id for showservicecontrolpolicy).

steps.name: delete-service-control-policies
steps._note: Collection DELETE (id list body). Guarded; body shape 'ids' UNPROVEN.

lifecycles._note: Policy bindings ATTACH an SCP to a target (org/OU/account) — this is the step that ACTIVATES a deny policy and can lock the hierarchy. Delegation policies grant a member account delegated admin over org resources. Both are high blast radius. COVERAGE-ONLY guarded calls: optional + broad expect_status, expected 403 on a non-master account, so no real binding/delegation is created. policy_id/target ids soft-captured read-only. Bodies UNPROVEN. Covers: attachpolicybindings(POST assignments/policy-bindings), removepolicybindings(DELETE assignments/policy-bindings), createdelegationpolicy(POST), setdelegationpolicy(PUT), deletedelegationpolicy(DELETE).

steps.name: attach-policy-bindings
steps._note: ATTACHING a deny SCP is what activates enforcement. Guarded — expected 403; body UNPROVEN. Placed after its soft-captured inputs so it isolates if either is missing.

steps.name: remove-policy-bindings
steps._note: Detach binding (id body). Guarded; body UNPROVEN.

steps.name: create-delegation-policy
steps._note: Delegates org admin to a member account. Guarded; body UNPROVEN.

lifecycles._note: Invitations bring an external account into THIS organization (or invite this account into another). Accepting/declining changes real membership. COVERAGE-ONLY guarded calls: optional + broad expect_status, expected 403/400/404 on a shared account, so no real invitation is sent or membership changed. invitation_id soft-captured from the read-only account-invitations/organization-invitations lists. Bodies UNPROVEN. Covers: createinvitation(POST), cancelinvitations(PUT cancel), acceptinvitation(PUT id/accept), declineinvitation(PUT id/decline).

steps.name: list-account-invitations
steps._note: Second read-only id source (this account's inbound invitations); overrides invitation_id when present.

steps.name: create-invitation
steps._note: Sends an org invitation. Guarded — expected 403 on a non-master account; body UNPROVEN. NOT chained to accept (we never alter real membership).

steps.name: cancel-invitations
steps._note: Cancel pending invitations (id list body). Guarded; body UNPROVEN.

steps.name: accept-invitation
steps._note: Accepting joins THIS account to another org — changes real membership. Guarded; expected 404/400 (no pending invitation) or 403.

steps.name: decline-invitation
steps._note: Decline a pending invitation. Guarded; expected 404/400 when none pending.
